prompts.description: Strengthen generate-meal-plan prompt to enforce ingredient-recipe consistency
prompts.prompt: In app/api/generate-meal-plan/route.ts, strengthen the system/user prompt sent to Claude to enforce ingredient-recipe consistency. Add an explicit rule (in both English and Korean prompt variants) stating: 'CRITICAL: Every ingredient mentioned in the recipe.instructions array MUST appear in the meal.ingredients array with a quantity. If a recipe step mentions kimchi, onion, soy sauce, or any other food item, that exact item must be listed in ingredients. Do not reference ingredients in instructions that are not in the ingredients array.' Place this rule prominently near the top of the meal generation instructions, before the JSON schema example. Do NOT change the JSON response shape, model, max_tokens, timeout, or the MEAL_ROTATION logic. Do NOT touch adjustMacros() or other post-processing. Verify: npm run build passes and a sample request still returns the same DayPlan[] structure.

Create a new exported function `validateIngredientConsistency(meal: Meal): { valid: boolean; missing: string[] }` in lib/meal-validator.ts. The function should: (1) extract candidate food nouns from meal.recipe.instructions by lowercasing and scanning for any ingredient name that appears in a built-in common food dictionary (include at minimum: kimchi, rice, egg, eggs, tuna, chicken, beef, pork, tofu, onion, garlic, soy sauce, sesame oil, olive oil, salt, pepper, butter, cheese, milk, yogurt, oats, bread, pasta, noodles, broccoli, spinach, carrot, potato, tomato, cucumber, lettuce, avocado, banana, apple, 김치, 밥, 계란, 참치, 닭, 소고기, 돼지고기, 두부, 양파, 마늘, 간장, 참기름); (2) check each detected food against meal.ingredients[].name (case-insensitive substring match both ways); (3) return missing ingredients. Do NOT modify the existing validateMealPlan or validateSwapCandidate functions' signatures — add this as a new standalone function. Export it. Verify: npm run build passes and the function is importable from lib/meal-validator.ts.

prompts.description: Add consistency check + one-retry loop to generate-meal-plan route
prompts.prompt: In app/api/generate-meal-plan/route.ts, after Claude returns the meal plan and after the existing adjustMacros() post-processing, add an ingredient consistency check. Import validateIngredientConsistency from lib/meal-validator. For each meal in each day, call validateIngredientConsistency(meal). If ANY meal returns valid: false, log the missing ingredients server-side (console.warn with meal name and missing list), then make ONE retry call to Claude asking specifically to regenerate only the failing meals with a prompt like: 'The following meals have recipe instructions that reference ingredients not in their ingredients array. Regenerate ONLY these meals so that every ingredient mentioned in instructions is also in the ingredients array with a quantity: [list of meal names and missing ingredients].' Replace the failing meals in the weekPlan with the retry results. If retry still fails for some meals, keep the original and log a warning — do not fail the whole request. Do NOT add a second retry. Do NOT change the 35s timeout or maxDuration. Verify: npm run build passes and existing happy-path behavior is unchanged when all meals are consistent.

prompts.description: Apply consistency check as drop-filter to swap-meal route
prompts.prompt: Apply the same ingredient consistency check to app/api/swap-meal/route.ts. After Claude returns swap candidates and after the existing validateSwapCandidate filtering, run validateIngredientConsistency on each remaining candidate. Drop any candidate where valid: false (do not retry — swap already returns up to 3 candidates, so dropping one is acceptable). If all 3 are dropped, return an empty candidates array with a clear error message in the response so the client can show 'No valid swaps found, try again'. Do NOT change the API response shape ({ candidates: SwapCandidate[] }) beyond this. Do NOT modify validateSwapCandidate itself. Verify: npm run build passes.
